architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta from base_model.py: one attention head, token embedding + Q/K projections, softmax, no MLP — directly answers the span question.

baseline_comparison.note: Positional ALiBi strawman (finite, decaying span) and uniform baseline both fail where the content head holds flat at 0.997.

faithfulness.note: Ablation zeroing the needle key direction collapses attention exactly to uniform, causally isolating the Q/K alignment as the source of span.

operating_range.note: Attention stays flat ~0.997 across d=1..256 (>2 orders of magnitude), robustness ratio ~1.0.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set weights (build_head) reproduce the target behaviour — clean demonstration of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Single log2-distance chart overlays content (flat-high), positional (decaying), ablation (pinned to baseline) plus an interactive ALiBi-slope slider — claim is legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties log2 axis, uniform baseline reference, and three-curve contrast to exactly what the goal asks about span.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built single-head content-retrieval circuit that answers the goal directly: span is bounded by content addressing, not distance, holding ~0.997 attention flat across 1..256. It pairs a decaying ALiBi positional strawman with a key-direction ablation that collapses to uniform, giving both a baseline contrast and genuine causal evidence. The visualisation cleanly overlays all three curves on a log2 axis with an interactive span-shrinking slider; every rubric item is satisfied.
